questions.answer: JavaScript is a programming language used to create dynamic and interactive content on web pages.
JavaScript runs in browsers, servers (Node.js), and tools like VS Code.
console.log() prints output or messages to the browser console for debugging.

questions.q: What is the difference between let and const?
questions.answer: let allows reassignment, while const does not allow reassignment after initialization.
typeof is used to check the data type of a variable.

questions.q: Explain how HTML, CSS, and JavaScript work together.
questions.answer: HTML provides structure, CSS provides styling, and JavaScript adds interactivity to web pages.

questions.q: What are primitive data types in JavaScript?
questions.answer: Primitive data types include number, string, boolean, null, undefined, symbol, and bigint.
== compares values with type conversion, while === compares both value and type strictly.
Logical operators (&&, ||, !) are used to combine or invert boolean conditions.

questions.q: What are truthy and falsy values?
questions.answer: Truthy values evaluate to true, while falsy values (false, 0, '', null, undefined, NaN) evaluate to false.

questions.q: Write a JavaScript program demonstrating variables, data types, operators, and type conversion.
Write a program using if-else and switch to display grade based on marks.

Write a JavaScript program using loops and functions to:
1. Calculate factorial of a number
2. Print multiplication table
3. Use a function to return result
